stories.title: A complex set of negotiations to end Israel's overlapping wars
stories.description: Israel has agreed to a ceasefire in Lebanon, part of a complex web of Mideast negotiations, from Iran to Gaza.

stories.title: Iran War timeline continues to shift. And, jury rules Live Nation acted as monopoly
stories.description: Trump continues to try to declare victory in Iran as the timeline for the war's end shifts. And, a jury determined that Live Nation acted as a monopoly and overcharged ticket buyers.

stories.title: Israel agrees to a ceasefire in its war with Hezbollah in Lebanon, Trump says
stories.description: The leaders of Israel and Lebanon have agreed on a 10-day ceasefire in Israel's fight against Iran-backed Hezbollah in Lebanon, according to President Trump.

stories.title: 3 things to know about naval blockades as the U.S. patrols the Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: The White House says it wants to choke off Iran's oil export revenue. But experts say that blockades are often unpredictable and difficult to enforce.

stories.title: Lyse Doucet: Under fragile ceasefire, Iranians wonder if US deal can be done
stories.description: The BBC's chief international correspondent reports from Iran as diplomatic efforts to avoid a return to war intensify.

stories.title: Israeli triple-tap strike kills three paramedics in Lebanon, officials say
stories.description: Lebanon's government condemns as a "flagrant crime" the killing of the paramedics, one of whom featured in a BBC report.

stories.title: Israel and Hezbollah continue attacks after Israel-Lebanon talks in US
stories.description: Israel strikes hit southern Lebanon and Hezbollah fires rockets at Israel, a day after rare direct negotiations.

stories.title: Sri Lanka repatriates 238 Iranian sailors stranded after US torpedo attack
stories.description: The survivors of the US attack on Iranian vessel Iris Dena, which claimed the lives of 104 people, were among those sent home.

stories.title: Trump hints Iran talks could resume this week as US port blockade continues
stories.description: The United Nations' secretary general said it was "highly probable" that the negotiations would restart.

stories.title: Lebanon seeks peace, but Hezbollah needs to be convinced first
stories.description: The Lebanese government go into peace talks with limited influence over the group.

stories.title: The Israeli town on the frontline with Hezbollah
stories.description: BBC foreign correspondent Nick Beake visits Metula, an Israeli town surrounded on three sides by Lebanon.

stories.title: Israel and Lebanon hold first direct talks since 1993
stories.description: A US statement said the two sides had agreed to launch direct negotiations, at a time and place to be determined.

stories.title: How many ships are crossing the Strait of Hormuz?
stories.description: Since the start of the US blockade on Monday, 15 vessels have crossed the Strait of Hormuz, nine of which have links to Iran, BBC Verify analysis of ship-tracking data suggests.

stories.title: Why and how is US blockading Iranian ports in Strait of Hormuz?
stories.description: Donald Trump says that the US is blockading the Strait of Hormuz. What does this mean in practice?

stories.title: 'Lebanon is being held hostage by Hezbollah acting at Iran's behest'
stories.description: Hezbollah plunged Lebanon into war with Israel in early March. Six weeks later, the group, which had suffered setbacks in 2024, is still putting up a fight. But how? And can the Lebanese government succeed in getting the militant group to disarm? In this edition of Middle East Ma...

stories.title: Live: Trump says Israel and Lebanon have agreed to a 10-day ceasefire
Lebanon president had phone call with Trump, thanked him for 'efforts' towards ceasefire
stories.description: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun held a phone call with Donald Trump on Thursday during which he thanked the US leader for his "efforts" to secure a ceasefire with Israel, the presidency in Beirut said. The call comes after Aoun rejected a US request for a "direct call" with Israel...

stories.description: Angela Diffley is pleased to welcome Bilal Y. Saab, Senior Managing Director of TRENDS US.Former Pentagon Official in the first Trump administration. According to Saab, Lebanon is at a crossroads: the government is committed to reform, is fundamentally “pro-Lebanese” and rejects ...

stories.title: Lebanon's Aoun will not hold call with Netanyahu in near future, Lebanese officials say
stories.description: Lebanon's ⁠President Joseph ​Aoun will not ​hold a call with Israeli Prime Minister ​Benjamin Netanyahu ‌in ⁠the near future, ‌three Lebanese officials told Reuters ⁠on Thursday, after US President Donald ​Trump said leaders ‌of both countries would speak. Two of ‌the Lebanese of...

stories.title: FRANCE 24 report: Lebanon’s displaced turn to private aid groups as state support struggles
stories.description: As the war continues in Lebanon, so do the waves of displacement. Thousands of families are leaving their homes, and searching for temporary shelter in a country already straining under the weight of war. Government aid is limited, so private groups are stepping in to fill the ga...

stories.title: REPLAY: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth leads press conference on Iran war
stories.description: The United States will blockade Iranian ports for "as long as it takes," US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Thursday, threatening renewed strikes if Tehran does not make a deal. "If Iran chooses poorly, then they will have a blockade and bombs dropping on infrastructure, powe...

stories.title: 10-day Lebanon-Israel ceasefire announced by Trump
stories.description: Israel and Lebanon have agreed to a 10-day ceasefire starting at 21:00 GMT on Thursday, said US President Donald Trump.

stories.title: No date set for US-Iran talks, as Pakistan pushes to keep diplomacy alive
stories.description: Pakistan's prime minister and army chief are both travelling to key players in the war, attempting to keep talks going.

stories.title: US general clarifies Iranian ports under blockade, not Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: A US general had to clarify that the US military is only blockading Iranian ports, not the entire Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Trump says Israel and Lebanon agree to temporary ceasefire
stories.description: Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam welcomes Donald Trump's announcement of 10-day truce that takes effect on Thursday.

stories.title: Jet fuel shortage: Why Iran war could ground flights in Europe
stories.description: Europe's aviation faces fuel shortages as Hormuz tensions threaten travel at the start of the summer season.

stories.title: Pentagon chief Hegseth warns Iran blockade to last ‘as long as takes’
stories.description: US urges resolution through talks, but says Washington 'locked and loaded' to resume attacks.

stories.title: US defence secretary Pete Hegseth attacks press coverage of war on Iran
stories.description: US defence secretary Hegseth criticised media coverage of the war on Iran.

stories.description: US President Donald Trump announced on Thursday that Lebanon and Israel had agreed on a 10-day ceasefire. Israel has been carrying out deadly attacks in Lebanon against the backdrop of a now-paused US-Israeli war on Iran. The war spilled into Lebanon on March 2, with an attack by...

stories.title: Outpouring of emotion as Turkiye buries school shooting victims
stories.description: Crowds gathered for the funerals of eight children and a teacher in southern Turkiye on Thursday, who were killed when a 14-year-old opened fire at a school in a crime that has plunged the country in to shock and the community into grief. As family members and mourners flocked ar...

stories.title: PM Shehbaz arrives in Turkiye on third leg of tri-nation tour
stories.description: After wrapping up visits to Saudi Arabia and Qatar, Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif on Thursday arrived in Turkiye’s Antalya on the third leg of his tri-nation tour, according to the Prime Minister’s Office (PMO) According to the Foreign Office (FO), the prime minister, accompanied...

stories.title: Amid Lebanon truce talks, Israel kills 3 paramedics
stories.description: Wael Sabbagh, whose mother Afaf Sidaoui and brother Hassan were killed in an Israeli strike on an apartment building, stands outside the site of the attack in Beirut.—Reuters BEIRUT: Amid efforts of a ceasefire in Lebanon, Israel continued its attacks and targeted paramedic teams...

stories.title: Turkiye launches internet crackdown ahead of funerals for shooting victims
stories.description: Turkiye cracked down on Thursday on social media posts praising two school shootings in two days, which have left the country reeling, ordering dozens of arrests ahead of funerals for the victims, who were mostly children. Authorities said 83 people had been detained, hundreds of...

stories.title: Fuel supply fears after blaze tears through crucial Australian refinery
stories.description: Columns of fire engulfed a crucial Australian oil refinery after a chain of explosions, authorities said on Thursday, as they warned of disruptions to domestic fuel supply. Flames as high as 60 metres erupted late on Wednesday after a gas leak ignited at the Viva fuel plant in Vi...

stories.title: Pakistan Army chief Munir holds talks with top Iranian leadership in Tehran
stories.description: Asim Munir discussed with the commander of the IRGC the steps taken under the framework for ending the war and their recent talks in Tehran.

stories.title: Iran-Israel war LIVE: Trump announces 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon

stories.title: Brazilian President Lula says Trump has ‘no right’ to threaten countries
stories.description: His comments come after Mr. Trump this month warned that a “whole civilisation will die” in Iran if the country did not open up the Strait of Hormuz

stories.title: Govt on “wait and watch” mode as U.S. denies extending waivers on Russian, Iranian crude purchases
stories.description: U.S.’ waiver for procuring of Iranian crude expires April 19, while that Russian oil expired April 11

stories.title: America is in a losing position in the Gulf, but Israel is winning: University of Oklahoma Professor Joshua Landis
stories.description: In an interview with The Hindu, Joshua Landis a professor at the University of Oklahoma, said U.S. President Donald Trump’s blockade of Iran is doomed to fail, but Israel, which doesn’t support the ceasefire with Iran, would try to drag the U.S. back into a conflict with Iran

stories.title: EU foreign ministers to discuss €90 billion for Kiev, 20th package of sanctions next week

stories.title: Medvedev asserts no one will ‘let Trump take all the oil everywhere’
stories.description: On March 30, the US leader said, speaking about Iran, that his "preference would be to take the oil"
